ground_truth.title: SMF vs MyBB? What are the differences? (looking for 2024 opinions)
ground_truth.author: StarredSkies
ground_truth.main_content:
StarredSkies
2019-02-23, 09:14 PM

I've read through a few of these threads but they've all been outdated. Apologies if this isn't the right spot!

As of right now, I run a SMF website and have been thinking of converting to MyBB. With SMF's release of 2.1, I have been dearly disappointed. If I were to upgrade, many of my modifications would fail to work (and yes, I've checked this). Subaccounts / post templates / profile coding and things will all cease to exist.

With that said, I've heard a lot of great things about MyBB among the years. The platform looks fantastic with many responsive layouts and great plugins... but I'm still unsure. What are MyBB's pros and cons? and how does it compare to SMF?

Thank you in advance.

User 37079
2019-02-24, 02:31 AM

Why not try it out, if able that is.

Haven't used SMF for a very long time, so can not help there.

StarredSkies
2019-02-24, 05:31 PM

The problem is that to test it, I'd most definitely have to transfer files over from my current website with the merge system. I have attempted a test site with MyBB on it on a subdomain, but it has not worked. Not exactly fluent on how to do these things... I'll figure it out later though!

SMF hasn't updated whatsoever in years. 2.1 was in development for years until this February! Nevertheless, your opinions would be nice.

vbgamer45
2019-02-24, 06:39 PM

If you have any issues please post we can help with the conversion.

dragonexpert
2019-02-25, 02:25 AM

The last time I used SMF was many years ago so it may have changed since then. Back when I used it, you could not place any kind of restrictions on admins at all. If they could access the admin panel, they could do everything. MyBB enables you to put permissions for each admin so they can only access what they need to do their job. There also wasn't really a warning system back then either.

Omar G.
2019-02-26, 12:38 AM

StarredSkies Wrote:
SMF hasn't updated whatsoever in years. 2.1 was in development for years until this February! Nevertheless, your opinions would be nice.

For me the development process is the most seriuos drawback. Last time I checked their features and usage were not too different from those of MyBB, but now that I can code almost anything I would like for MyBB the development spans for SMF are everything but acceptable and promising.

StarredSkies
2019-02-26, 01:13 AM

Thank you for the opinions! I'm definitely going to take a peek at preforming a merge on a subdomain with a duplicate of my current database. I have to test it first before going live, of course.

StarredSkies
2024-01-05, 03:34 AM

Heya! Sorry if this is considered necroposting, but I did not want to create a new thread if it wasn't necessary.

As you can see, I am back! I gave it some time and waited for 2.1 to catch up to time, but I am afraid the plugins I need are still outdated (and cost hundreds, maybe thousands, to be updated privately).

That said, I am looking for new opinions and your thoughts in the year 2024! This time I am looking for key differences that will clearly sway me to MyBB... staying with SMF 2.0 can only last so long. At the moment, my key concerns are:
- Account switching/subaccounts (Is this modification still being updated?)
- A shop system (Newpoints vs Mint)
- Post templates (BBcode-based templates)
- Responsive theme development
- Anything else that MyBB excels at in comparison to SMF (in your opinion)

What does everyone think? I am also looking into Xenforo, but that can be worth a pretty penny for a free website.

Omar G.
2024-01-09, 08:49 AM

StarredSkies Wrote:
- Account switching/subaccounts (Is this modification still being updated?)
- A shop system (Newpoints vs Mint)
- Post templates (BBcode-based templates)
- Responsive theme development

These are all existing plugins, which should all be relatively easy to update, if necessary. The 1.8 core base hasn't drastically changed on the past 10 or so years for old plugins to be seriously complicate to update.

doylecc
2024-01-09, 09:13 AM

StarredSkies Wrote:
- Account switching/subaccounts (Is this modification still being updated?)

It is, but there are only maintenance updates. No new features are planned. For the latest versions you can also check out this site
